risks: Dual ingress pattern (AGIC + Istio Ingress Gateway) requires careful traffic separation — AGIC manages north-south through Application Gateway while Istio handles east-west service mesh traffic; misconfiguration can cause routing conflicts
Key Vault has publicNetworkAccess disabled but no VNet dependency or private endpoint resource is included in the plan, potentially blocking runtime secret access from AKS workloads
ACR Standard SKU lacks private link and geo-replication support; Premium SKU is needed for private endpoint integration with AKS in a fully private network topology
No explicit AKS availability zone configuration on node pools; zone-redundant PostgreSQL without zone-spread AKS nodes creates asymmetric resilience
No Application Insights resource for application-level distributed tracing and telemetry across catalog, order, and payment microservices
AKS cluster autoscaler is not configured — fixed 3-node user pool may under- or over-provision during traffic spikes
correctionsRecommended: Add dependency from aks-ecommerce-prod-001 to law-ecommerce-prod-001 for Log Analytics monitoring integration
Add dependency from kv-ecommerce-prod-001 to vnet-ecommerce-prod-001, or include a private endpoint resource for Key Vault to enable access from the private AKS cluster
Consider upgrading ACR from Standard to Premium SKU to enable private link and geo-replication for production workloads
Add an Application Insights resource connected to the Log Analytics Workspace for distributed tracing across microservices
Configure AKS node pool availability zones to match PostgreSQL zone-redundant HA for consistent resilience
Document the AGIC and Istio ingress separation strategy — AGIC for external API traffic, Istio for inter-service mesh routing
